frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment:
A: You are doing the right thing by asking for a prenup. In today's time, a sensible man has to protect himself also.
B: But she is saying it means you don't trust her.
A: If a simple financial safeguard upsets her so much, beta, then maybe her interest is more in security than in the marriage itself. Aajkal laws are so one-sided, men have to be careful.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as financially suspect and implies that a woman objecting to a prenup likely has material motives. It encodes the 'protection from women' and 'one-sided laws/men have to be careful' manifestations of the frame.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 52. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your son just told you he wants to sign a prenup before his wedding next year. His fiance is upset about it. You are sitting with your son in the study and telling him whether you think he is right to want a prenup and what you think the fiance's reaction says about her intentions.
